tex:
\begin{question*}{9}{a}Tomas sells a computer, a bike and a phone.
The amounts he receives are in the ratio computer : bike : phone $= 14 : 17 : 9$.

\begin{subpartsaliged}
Calculate the amount he receives for the phone as a percentage of the total.

The total amount he receives is \$560.
Calculate how much he receives for the bike.

Tomas originally bought the bike for \$195.
He wanted to make a profit of at least 25\% when he sold it.

Does Tomas make a profit of at least 25\%?
You must show all your working to support your decision.

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b)Ulla invests \$725 for 6 years in an account paying simple interest at a rate of 1.3\% per year.

Calculate the total interest earned at the end of 6 years.

(c)In a sale, all prices are reduced by 24\%.
Victor pays \$36.86 for a pair of shoes in the sale.

Calculate the original price of the shoes.

\end{question*}

Anna records the number of text messages she receives for 14 days.

\begin{tabular}{ccccccc}
17 & 15 & 31 & 38 & 31 & 22 & 13 \\
18 & 21 & 27 & 28 & 21 & 31 & 29
\end{tabular}
(a) Complete the stem-and-leaf diagram.

\begin{tabular}{c|cccccc}
1 & 8  & & & & & \\
2 & 9  & & & & & \\
3 & 1  & & & & & \\
\end{tabular}

Key: ...

(b) Find the median.

(c) Find the mode.

(d) Find the range.

 **(b)** In a shop, there are 4 red and 8 grey phones.
Anna and Pete each pick one of these phones at random.

Work out the probability that they both pick a grey phone.

The scale drawing shows two sides, $AB$ and $BC$, of a field.
The scale is 5 centimetres represents 200 metres.

\begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

Scale: 5 cm to 200 m

(a) Measure angle $ABC$.

(b) $X$ is a point on $BC$.
$BX = 332$ m.

Mark the point $X$ on the diagram.

(c) Find the scale in the form $1 : n$.

 **(b)** A bronze statue is 4.5 m high and has a mass of 195\,200 kg.
The density of bronze is 8000 kg/m$^3$.
The volume of a mathematically similar model of the statue is $0.385$ m$^3$.

Calculate the height of the model.
[Density = Mass $\div$ Volume]

(a) The cumulative frequency table shows information about the speed of each of 200 cars as they pass a speed camera.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Speed ($v$ km/h) & $v \leqslant 70$ & $v \leqslant 80$ & $v \leqslant 90$ & $v \leqslant 95$ & $v \leqslant 100$ & $v \leqslant 120$ \\
\hline
Cumulative frequency & 12 & 46 & 115 & 155 & 177 & 200 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i) (b) On the grid, draw the cumulative frequency diagram.

\begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

(c) Use your cumulative frequency diagram to find an estimate of

\PartLabelInline{a} the median

\PartLabelInline{b} the interquartile range

\PartLabelInline{c} the number of cars with a speed greater than 110 km/h.

(d) The frequency table shows information about the mass of each of 50 trucks.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Mass ($m$ kg) & $2000 < m \leqslant 2600$ & $2600 < m \leqslant 3500$ & $3500 < m \leqslant 5000$ & $5000 < m \leqslant 5700$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 12 & 15 & 16 & 7 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i) (e) Calculate an estimate for the mean mass of the trucks.

(f) In a histogram showing this information, the height of the first block is 6 cm.

Calculate the heights of the remaining three blocks.

Height of block for $2600 < m \leqslant 3500$ 

Height of block for $3500 < m \leqslant 5000$ 

Height of block for $5000 < m \leqslant 5700$

tex:
(a) A shop sells shirts for $\$x$ and jackets for $\$(x + 27)$.
The shop sells 4 shirts and 3 jackets for a total of \$194.75.

Write down and solve an equation to find the cost of one shirt.

(b) Solve the simultaneous equations.
You must show all your working.

\[
\begin{aligned}
x^2 + 4y &= 37 \\
5x + y &= -8
\end{aligned}
\]

(c) A solid cylinder has radius $x$ and height $6x$.
A sphere of radius $r$ has the same surface area as the total surface area of the cylinder.

Show that $r^2 = \dfrac{7}{2} x^2$.
[The surface area, $A$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $A = 4\pi r^2$.]

The table shows some values for $y = 2^x - 3$.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
$x$ & $-2$ & $-1$ & 0 & 0.5 & 1 & 1.5 & 2 & 2.5 \\
\hline
$y$ & $-2.75$ & & & $-1.58$ & & $-0.17$ & 1 & 2.66 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) Complete the table.

(b) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 2^x - 3$ for $-2 \leqslant x \leqslant 2.5$.

\begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

(c) Use your graph to solve the equation $2^x - 3 = 2$.

(d) By drawing a suitable straight line, solve the equation $2^x - x - 1.5 = 0$.

(a) Complete the table. (b) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 2^x - 3$ for $-2 \leqslant x \leqslant 2.5$. (c) Use your graph to solve the equation $2^x - 3 = 2$. (d) By drawing a suitable straight line, solve the equation $2^x - x - 1.5 = 0$.

tex:
$M$ has coordinates $(4, 1)$ and $N$ has coordinates $(-2, -7)$.

(a) Find the length of $MN$.

(b) Find the gradient of $MN$.

(c) Find the equation of the perpendicular bisector of $MN$.

The equation of a curve is $y = x^4 - 8x^2 + 5$.

(a) Find the derivative, $\dfrac{\mathrm{d}y}{\mathrm{d}x}$, of $y = x^4 - 8x^2 + 5$.

(b) Find the coordinates of the three turning points.
You must show all your working.

(c) Determine which one of these turning points is a maximum.
Justify your answer.
